message: I've been reading up on power outages lately—turns out there's a lot of small, doable stuff we can think through *before* the lights go out, which honestly takes a lot of the stress out of it. The Red Cross has a really straightforward guide that walks through the basics without being heavy-handed about it. What's one thing in your home that would be trickiest to deal with during an outage—the fridge, staying warm, or something else?
